content.text: So, every day I earn at least $2,000
from passive income. And it is through
seven passive income ideas that I use to
earn money. And so, in this video, I'm
going to discuss what each of these
ideas are, their pros and cons, plus
show you ways that other people can get
started with them, too. And I think that
you might be surprised because I've
watched a lot of passive income videos
on YouTube, and there are ideas that I
use that no one else talks about. So
then, let's get started by talking about
my first passive income idea that I use
to earn money, which is automated
t-shirt stores. Yep, I've got lots of
videos on this channel discussing how I
run multiple automated clothing stores
with Shopify and Etsy. And although I no
longer just sell t-shirts, I sell other
clothes, too. T-shirts have by far made
me the most money. Because, you see,
here's the thing, right? If you come to
Etsy and you do a search for any sort of
basic clothing item, such as a t-shirt,
most of the t-shirts that you see here
are not being printed and shipped by the
Etsy seller themselves. Instead, the
vast majority are being made through two
of the most popular print on demand
apps, Printify and Printull. It's free
to register for an account on both, and
then you can go through their catalog of
blank items, and choose an item that
you'd like to customize, such as a
t-shirt. Then you create your t-shirt
design. For me, as my subscribers know,
I like to use a combination of AI Art
and Canva to create my t-shirt designs.
Then you just upload your design onto
the blank t-shirt. And then you connect
your Printify or Printull account to
your store. And both of these apps have
automated connections to all of the
major e-commerce platforms out there
such as Shopify, Amazon, and Etsy. And
then you can list your item for sale for
a markup price. And each time a customer
buys one of them from you, your print on
demand app will automatically see the
order. The print shop will then charge
your card on file the discounted price
for the t-shirt and then print your
design onto it, package it up and ship
it out while you get to keep the
difference as profit. So for me, I
personally find this very passive as my
print on demand apps take care of
fulfillment other than answering some
emails. There is very little bit I have
to actually do each day. So yes, that's
what I do. So let's now discuss the pros
and cons. And we'll start with the pros.
Why not? So firstly, I just really enjoy
it. I really enjoy creating the designs
with Canva and I really really love it
when I see someone wearing one of my
designs. And secondly, I like that it
doesn't take a lot of time. So you take
this bestselling t-shirt here, right? It
is a clever design. I love it. It is a
great t-shirt, but technically it isn't
a very complicated design. Like if I
come up with this idea myself, I could
have probably made a very similar design
in Canva in less than 30 minutes.
However, in saying all this, there are
two things that you need to consider.
Firstly, unless you have a credit card,
this is not a free side hustle to start.
And here's why. So, I sell my t-shirts
for around $21 with shipping as an extra
charge. So, in total, this customer here
paid me $25.94 for a t-shirt, and it
cost me $16.88
to buy and ship the t-shirt from
Printull. So, overall, it's a great
margin. However, it takes time for banks
to process the money. So, you're not
going to see that $25 in your bank
account for usually at least 2 to three
business days. So, you have two options
in the meantime. Firstly, if you have a
credit card, it is really easy. You can
just use your credit card to pay the $16
to Printull and then when the money
clears in your bank account, you can
just pay off the credit card. Or you can
use a debit card which has $16 on it
already. However, I understand not
everybody has a debit card with $16 on
it and not everybody has a credit card.
I totally get it. In addition, you're
also going to need to have a plan on how
you're going to market and advertise
your store. This is why most beginners
usually choose to pick a third-party
marketplace like Amazon or Etsy because
these marketplaces already market and
advertise themselves. And so, you get to
just piggy back off of their traffic and
not have to market products yourself.
And by the way, if you're curious and
want to see a case study where I started
a successful store from scratch on Etsy
using their free traffic, then I highly
recommend checking out this video. Plus,
you can get a copy of my free ebook, The
Six Steps That SixFigure Online Stores
Follow to Make Over $10,000 a month with
print on demand. In it, I share my
personal tips and tricks. So, if you
want a copy, there'll be a link to
download it in the description. But
anyway, back to the video. And this
leads me on to my next passive income
idea, which is to create and sell
digital products on Etsy. So, this is
something that I'm just getting into,
but basically on Etsy, you will find
lots of digital products for sale, too,
not just physical products. And the way
these work is interesting. So, when a
customer buys one of these, rather than
having an item physically mailed to
them, they instead have an email sent to
them from Etsy, which contains a
download link that they can click on and
then download their digital product. So,
this is a very passive income idea when
you think about it because Etsy
basically does everything for you. They
advertise and they drive traffic to
their marketplace and they do website
maintenance on your store and they even
send the customer your digital product.
Basically, there is very little that a
seller actually needs to do dayto-day.
And the most popular digital products
tend to fall into one of three
categories. So, the first are notion
templates. People love buying
aesthetically pleasing notion templates.
For example, you take this planner here,
right? Designed to help women with ADHD.
You can see it has had seven sales
today. And secondly, you have printable
PDFs. And probably the most popular
printable PDFs are decorations such as
printable posters or charts or
invitations, that sort of thing. Like
take this printable poster set here as
an example. You can see that it has had
eight sales today. And then thirdly, you
have Canva templates. So that is when
you create a design in Canva and then
share it with customers as a template
link. So that way they can customize it
themselves. For example, this Canva
template is a bundle of editable wedding
signs. And you can see that it's had 13
sales today. So then let's discuss the
pros and cons. And so probably the
biggest pro that I see with this is that
unlike with selling physical products,
it is completely free to create and sell
a digital product. After all, unlike
with my t-shirt business, you don't have
to buy a product to resell it. Instead,
you just create your digital product and
then it's free to make another copy of
it to sell. Secondly, again, if you're
just a creative person, then you might
find this a lot of fun. If you enjoy
creating notion templates or PDF files
or Canva templates, then you might
genuinely enjoy doing this. The biggest
downside though that I see is that
unlike with say a basic slogan t-shirt,
it's a lot more work to create these.
Like for example, that t-shirt before,
the creator just had to create one
single design and then slap it on a
t-shirt. But for this one here, this
wedding sign bundle requires the creator
to have made 70 different designs to be
able to sell them as a pack. And so, as
a whole, I think that digital products
require more upfront work to create and
sell. And I'll tell you what, all of
this talk about physical products versus
digital products reminds me of the next
passive income idea on my list, which is
credit card cash outs. Because you see,
here's the thing, right? To make money
with my automated t-shirt stores, I have
to spend money to make money, right?
Because I've got to buy the t-shirts
that I resell. And so, to pay for them,
I use credit cards. And so, since I sell
a lot of clothes, I get a lot of credit
card reward points. And for me, my
favorite card that I use is the MX
Platinum. This allows me to exchange my
credit card points for cash, and it
allows me to exchange my points for
business class and first class flights
for me and my family, which is my
personal favorite way to use them. So,
let's discuss the pros and cons. And
honestly, this one is pretty simple. If
you're running a business or an online
store that has to spend money to make
money, then this is an absolute
nobrainer. This is just a really easy
passive way to earn extra cash. But
since the best credit cards with the
best rewards usually have yearly fees,
then if you're running a free business
like a digital product store, then it's
probably not worth it to get them. All
right, then. So, the next passive income
idea on my list is this evergreen
videos. Yes. So, Tik Tok and YouTube
have pretty good monetization programs.
Sadly, Facebook, Instagram, and Snapchat
don't really have proper ones right now.
But at the very least, we still have
YouTube and Tik Tok, which are great
because they split ad revenue with their
creators. So, for me, of course, I take
advantage of the YouTube monetization
program. When people watch my videos,
ads are also shown alongside them. So,
YouTube splits the ad revenue with me.
However, if you want to earn passive
money doing this, then I strongly
strongly recommend that you focus on
evergreen content rather than trendy
topics for your videos. Basically,
evergreen content is when you make a
video which people are likely to watch
months, if not years into the future,
usually because it is educational and
helpful. So, take this video here that I
made 3 years ago. It's a tutorial for
Canva on how to create a t-shirt design.
And well, if you think about it, people
are always creating t-shirt designs
every day within Canva. So, this video
is just as relevant today as it was
three years ago. And so, people keep
watching it. And so, then you can check
out my earnings for it. You can see that
in the past 3 years, every day, this
video has gone on to earn me anywhere
from on average $5 to $20 a day
passively. Whereas, on the flip side,
take this video here. It was a news
announcement related to a print on
demand website called Zazzle. Now, at
first the news was hot and so it made a
bunch of money, but now I am lucky if it
earns even 5 cents a day. So, depending
on what video topic you choose, YouTube
can be passive or not passive. If I was
choosing to run a news YouTube channel,
then I would have to be uploading videos
basically every day because my previous
videos would be out of date fast. I'd
have to be constantly replacing them
with new content. But since I've made a
lot of evergreen videos, even if I don't
upload a new video for a long time, I
still earn passive income every day, no
matter what. And as I said, YouTube is
not the only platform that pays
creators. Tik Tok has their own
monetization program. They pay eligible
creators on average 20 cents to $2 per
1,000 views on eligible videos that are
1 minute or longer. And honestly, a lot
of people prefer making videos for Tik
Tok because for some people, they find
it much easier to create a quick video
on their phone than to make a proper
YouTube video. So then, let's discuss
the pros and cons. And so again, if
you're a creative person, this can be a
lot of fun and it can be free to start.
These days, there are lots of great free
video editing apps like ClipChamp and
Cap Cut. And you can even just use your
phone as your video camera. But on the
flip side, compared to a t-shirt which
can take me just minutes to create, it
can take me days to create a YouTube
video. So do keep that in mind. And
speaking of videos, this also leads me
on to my next passive income idea, which
is affiliate marketing. As this is
another way that I earn money from
YouTube. You see in that Canva tutorial
video, right, there is a link that you
can click on and then you can go and
sign up for a subscription to Canva.
Well, this is an affiliate link which is
basically a referral link which tracks
whether somebody clicks on a link and
then goes and buys something and then if
they do, I get a commission. I have
tutorials on this channel for all of my
favorite apps and websites. So, combined
all of these videos have added up to a
nice stream of passive income each day
from affiliate commissions. Now, people
often ask me, Sarah, how can I find
affiliate links to promote products that
I like? And it's a good question. And
well, for me, I primarily use this
website here, Impact. Most of my
favorite brands have affiliate programs
which are run through Impact's website.
In addition though, I know that a lot of
people make a lot of money on YouTube
and Tik Tok through Amazon's affiliate
program. This is called Amazon
Associates. It lets you sign up and
create affiliate links directly to
Amazon, and you get paid when people
click them and buy something. And so a
lot of people make money on Tik Tok and
YouTube by creating educational or
helpful videos about products such as
review videos or unboxing videos. So
then let's discuss the pros and cons.
And probably the biggest pro of all is
that you can make money with this with
very little following. Because you see,
here's the thing, right? To stop
scammers from spamming YouTube, they had
to introduce minimum monetization
requirements. And so now to turn ads on,
you need to have at least 1,000 YouTube
subscribers. And on Tik Tok, you need
10,000 followers. However, what is
really cool is that before I even had
1,000 subscribers on YouTube, I was
already making money with this channel
because of affiliate marketing. On the
flip side, though, I understand that not
everybody wants to create tutorials or
helpful videos on products or apps, and
I absolutely get it. And so if you would
rather just create fun and entertaining
videos on any topic that you like, then
it might be better just to focus on ad
revenue. Okay, then let's now move on to
the next passive income idea on my list.
And this time it is one which I have
seen pretty much no one talk about here
on YouTube, which is leveraging tax
money. Because you see, here's the
thing, right? In most countries, when
you work a job and you get paid salary
or you get paid wages, tax money is
automatically deducted and withheld from
that money and it is paid directly to
the government and you never see the tax
money in your bank account. Whereas,
when you run your own business or you're
self-employed, you get to keep all of
the money. And then two to four times a
year depending on where you live, you
then pay taxes on the profits of your
business and you pay it in a single lump
sum. This means then financially you are
at a huge advantage compared to people
that are just earning money from working
a job because what you can do is you can
leverage that tax money to earn money
from it in the meantime. So you take me
here right in New Zealand I currently
pay tax three times a year. And so while
I'm holding that money in preparation of
paying it, I leverage it in multiple
ways with one of the easiest being just
putting it into a high yield savings
account and then earning interest from
it. So yeah, there really isn't any cons
to this. If you are self-employed or
running your business, this is just an
easy way to earn additional money.
Probably the only con to this is that
you can't do it if the only way you're
earning money is by working a job. So
then let's move on to the next passive
income idea that I earn money from,
which is automated emails. And again,
this is another idea that I don't think
I've seen anyone else talk about. So
remember how earlier I was like, "Hey,
do you want to learn the tips and tricks
that I personally use to earn money with
print on demand?" Well, if you do, you
can download a copy of my free ebook.
Well, there are two reasons why I made
this ebook. Firstly, at the time a lot
of people just wanted me to create a
free beginner guide to print on demand.
And so creating an ebook was the easiest
and simplest way for me to do that. But
secondly, to get my ebook, you have to
agree to sign up to my email newsletter,
which is great because this way if
YouTube ever shuts down, then I'm still
connected to my subscribers through
emails. But something that me and some
of my staff members have done is so that
we don't have to write emails for this
newsletter every single week, we have
created a series of educational emails
which contain links to helpful videos
which automatically get emailed out to
new subscribers. And these are called
email autoresponders. So for example, on
day 121 of subscribing, subscribers will
automatically get emailed this email
here. It previews a YouTube video that I
created which reviews the three most
popular print on demand apps. And of
course, it includes a link to where
people can go and watch the video. And
of course, whenever anyone clicks the
link to watch the video, I earn passive
money from YouTube ads by having viewers
come back to my channel. So then, what
are your thoughts on my passive income
ideas? Let me know in the comments. And
if you'd like to learn about even more
ways that people are earning money
online, be sure to watch my next video
here on screen. and I will see you in
the next video.
